segments.text: Some people are predicting that the future of video editing is going to be
agintic. What that means is that we, people, won't be editing our video, but AI
tools like cloud will do everything from cutting out silences to adding
transitions to adding cool zooms, B-roll, etc. The thing is, that future is
already here. Just last week I went viral on Twitter by sharing a clipping
tool that turns long-form content into short-form content. I made that in an
hour using cloud. My video on remotion where you can use cloud to
generate motion graphics and you no longer need motion graphic skills to add
to your videos is now at 20,000 views. And some of my favorite creators like Renee
are already editing their YouTube videos using cloud. Renee is actually the one
who put me on to using cloud for video editing. I've since personalized my
workflow a little bit, but credits do where it's due. In this video I'm
sharing my end-to-end workflow using cloud to edit my videos. I'll go over
how it does things like cutting silences, ums and as. It'll find places to
add b-roll as well as generate said b-roll either using tools like c-dance or
in our case, remotion. We're going to create motion graphics. I'll show you how you
can easily add photos, sounds, etc. without going into your editor and
actually finding the spots to add them. And I'll hint at how you can turn
longer-form content into social short-form content. You'll also learn
how in this age of agentic editing you can avoid all of your videos
looking the same as other people's videos by making sure that you use
sounds that only you know or memes that are really niche or
